description: Recommended database schema that accommodates all US state sports betting revenue data

columns.ggr_definition: TEXT -- how this state defines GGR

monthly_data.description: Main data table — one row per state/period/operator/channel/sport combination

columns.operator_raw: VARCHAR(200) -- name exactly as reported by state
columns.operator_standard: VARCHAR(100) -- normalized name from mapping table
columns.sport_category: VARCHAR(50) -- NULL if state doesn't break down by sport
columns.gross_revenue: BIGINT -- handle minus payouts, before promo deductions, in cents
columns.promo_credits: BIGINT -- free bets / promotional credits in cents, NULL if not reported
columns.net_revenue: BIGINT -- after promo deductions (taxable base in most states), in cents
columns.payouts: BIGINT -- winnings paid to bettors, NULL if not reported, in cents
columns.hold_pct: DECIMAL(6,4) -- gross_revenue / handle, NULL if not calculable
columns.tax_paid: BIGINT -- actual tax remitted, in cents
columns.num_bets: INTEGER -- number of wagers, NULL if not reported
columns.federal_excise_tax: BIGINT -- NULL if not reported separately, in cents

indexes: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_monthly_unique ON monthly_data(state_code, period_end, COALESCE(operator_standard,''), channel, COALESCE(sport_category,''))
CREATE INDEX idx_monthly_state_period ON monthly_data(state_code, period_end)
CREATE INDEX idx_monthly_operator ON monthly_data(operator_standard, period_end)
monthly_data.notes: Store currency as cents (BIGINT) to avoid floating point issues. Convert to dollars in application layer. Use COALESCE in unique index for nullable sport_category.

operator_aliases.description: Maps raw operator names to standardized names

scrape_log.description: Audit log for data collection runs

derived_views.v_yoy_handle_change:
handle - LAG(handle) OVER (
                    PARTITION BY state_code, operator_standard, channel
                    ORDER BY period_end
                ) AS handle_change_vs_prior_period
derived_views.v_market_share: handle::NUMERIC / NULLIF(SUM(handle) OVER (PARTITION BY state_code, period_end), 0) AS market_share

columns_that_vary_by_state: promo_credits -- some states don't report this at all (TN, SD, MT)
payouts -- only ~60% of states report raw payouts separately
sport_category -- only IL, NJ, LA, MO, CO, SD, MT, MS, MD have sport breakdown
num_bets -- very few states (none in standard reports)
federal_excise_tax -- only AZ, ME, KY break this out
channel -- some states are online-only (TN, NC, WY, VT), some don't split
data_quality_notes: TN taxes handle (not GGR), so gross_revenue field will be NULL for TN
NY promo credits are NOT deductible, so net_revenue = gross_revenue for NY
RI/NH/MT/OR are lottery monopolies - single operator per state
NV embeds sports in broader gaming report - sports data isolation may be approximate
MO allows 25% promo deduction, causing negative AGR in early months
IL graduated tax tiers (20-40%) + per-wager surcharge starting July 2025
